claim: An authored-spec requirement's link to code shall carry two ORTHOGONAL fields, never collapsed into one: implemented_by names WHERE the requirement is EMBODIED (a path-qualified file:symbol reference into the domain's authored spec/ tree, the model/method that realizes the claim), and verified_by names WHERE the requirement is PROVEN (a path-qualified file:test reference, the executable test that demonstrates the claim holds). A symbol that embodies a requirement without a test that proves it, or a test cited without the symbol it exercises, is an incomplete SpecLink -- ENFORCED status requires both halves together (see R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link), but the two fields answer two DIFFERENT questions and shall never be merged into a single field or treated as aliases of one another.
why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §4 ("Схема связи: requirement -> code -> test") and §12 (resolver decision 2026-07-16) establish implemented_by/verified_by as two separate fields on Requirement (internal/ontology/requirement.go), landed additively in task #223 and already carrying inline doc comments naming the same embodied/proven distinction (requirement.go:80-96: "ImplementedBy names WHERE this requirement is embodied", "VerifiedBy names WHERE this requirement is PROVEN"). R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked (#223) and R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link (#223) already model the MECHANICAL checking of these fields (symbol/test resolvability, non-vacuousness, no-skip, no-reuse) and the DISJUNCTIVE ENFORCED gate that accepts either enforced_by or implemented_by+verified_by together -- but neither requirement states the CONCEPTUAL distinction itself: why two fields exist, what each one MEANS, and why they are not interchangeable. Without this requirement as a first-class node, the SpecLink concept (PLAN §4's requirement->code->test schema) exists only in code comments and PLAN prose, not as a graph anchor a future agent can cite when modeling a business domain's own spec/ tree (task #224 pilot, task #236 recursion into hotam-spec-self's own engine code). This requirement is the missing OBJECT-level node: it names the SpecLink concept and its two fields' distinct semantics, one layer above the two already-landed mechanical-check requirements which govern HOW the fields are validated, not WHAT they mean. PROSE/SETTLED is correct here: this is a naming-and-definition claim about a data shape already implemented (internal/ontology/requirement.go ImplementedBy/VerifiedBy, task #223) -- the fields themselves are code, but THIS requirement's own implemented_by/verified_by linkage into hotam-spec-self's engine is deliberately deferred to task #236 (recursion), per the session's explicit instruction not to add code links at the objects+fields step.
evidence: internal/ontology/requirement.go:80-96 (ImplementedBy/VerifiedBy field doc comments, task #223) already state the embodied-vs-proven distinction in code; this requirement promotes it to a graph anchor
